summary: Lead converted product-owner UI/map feedback and visual thread REQUEST_CHANGES_UI_EXPERIENCE into an M7 UX recovery task graph. Formal content lock remains rejected/pending; autonomous work may continue only on these scoped repair tasks before content lock can be reconsidered.

tests_run.result: Read latest visual thread feedback and incorporated must-do M7 recovery items.

decisions.decision: Formal M7 content lock is not approved; repair tasks must complete before re-opening acceptance.
Start M7-MAP-SITUATION-MODE-UX-001 first because map readability and rectangular-grid replacement are the clearest product-owner blocker.

Scope is low-fidelity but playable UX: strategic map readability, first-screen orientation, decision inspector, task rail, and validation. No final art, server, telemetry, cloud-save, release/commercial, or manual node battle scope.

risks.summary: The recovery changes are product-facing and must retain sim/read-model authority boundaries and independent review.

requested_action: Validate task graph, integrate process branch, then start M7-MAP-SITUATION-MODE-UX-001 with client_engineer and systems_architect review.
